title: PhD Research Fellowship in Political Science at the University of Oslo: A Comprehensive Opportunity for Emerging Scholars
summary: The University of Oslo (UiO), Norway’s oldest and highest‑ranked research institution, is inviting outstanding candidates to apply for a 3–4‑year PhD Research Fellowship in Political Science . This prestigious opportunity is hosted by the Department of Political Science—Norway’s largest and most diverse political science department—offering a vibrant academic environment, world‑class research groups, and a strong commitment to diversity and inclusion. This fellowship is designed for highly motivated individuals who aspire to pursue advanced research across any sub‑field of political science, including comparative politics, public policy and administration, political theory, international relations, and research methods. As stated in the document: “We invite applications from excellent candidates in all sub-fields of political science…” About the Position The fellowship is a fixed-term, full-time appointment of 3 years , with the possibility of extension to 4 years if the candidate undertakes teaching or other qualifying duties. The position is embedded within the Faculty of Social Sciences’ PhD programme, and the selected candidate is expected to complete a PhD degree in political science during the fellowship period. Key structural features include: Integration into UiO’s structured PhD programme Access to career guidance and development support Requirement to submit a career development plan within the first month Opportunity to engage in teaching and departmental activities Participation in research groups and seminar series across multiple political science sub-fields The department emphasizes strategic career development, stating: “All PhD fellows are expected to submit a career development plan… no later than one month after commencement.” Qualification Requirements Formal Academic Requirements Applicants must meet the following academic standards: Education equivalent to five years of university study in Norway , including: A two-year (120 ECTS) Master’s degree in a relevant field A Master’s thesis worth at least 30 ECTS The Master’s degree must include training in research methods Other Required Qualifications Strong command of written and spoken English Excellent academic grades and a high-quality Master’s thesis A well-developed research proposal that clearly outlines: Research questions Contribution to existing literature Theoretical and methodological approach Planned dissertation format (article-based or monograph) A realistic timeline The document emphasizes that: “Top-tier grades and a highly commendable MA thesis are generally prerequisites for shortlisting.” Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion, and Accessibility UiO is deeply committed to fostering an inclusive academic environment. The university encourages applications from individuals with diverse backgrounds, including immigrants, ethnic minorities, and persons with disabilities. Adjustments can be made to accommodate individual needs, such as: Modified working hours Task adaptations Digital, technical, or physical accommodations As stated: “We will do our best to accommodate your needs… modifications to working hours, task adaptations… or other practical measures.” What the University of Oslo Offers Successful candidates will enjoy a wide range of benefits, including: Annual salary: NOK 550,800 – 595,000 Personal research funds: NOK 35,000 per year A stimulating international research environment A friendly, inclusive workplace with strong work-life balance Access to Norway’s world-class welfare system, including: Parental leave Affordable childcare Public services Courses and workshops on: Presentation skills Op-ed writing Stress management Financial and practical support for international staff relocating to Norway Potential tax reductions for new international employees Annual career development conversations Introduction course for PhD fellows How to Apply Applications must be submitted through UiO’s electronic recruitment system. Required documents include: Cover letter (max 2 pages) Research proposal (max 3,000 words, including references and footnotes) Curriculum Vitae (CV) Master’s thesis Transcripts and diplomas Explanation of grading system (for foreign applicants) Names and contact details of 2–3 referees The application must be submitted before the deadline . Application Deadline: 1 September 2026 The evaluation process begins immediately after the deadline and typically takes 3–4 months . The expected start date is January 2027 , subject to negotiation. About the University of Oslo and the Department of Political Science The University of Oslo is a leading European institution with: 28,000 students 7,000 employees Over 200 years of academic excellence The Department of Political Science hosts: Multiple research groups (CIR, PBO, OPT, PODS, CITIZENS) Interdisciplinary centres such as the Center for Research on Extremism (C-REX) Large BA and MA programmes A specialized MA in Peace and Conflict Studies For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post PhD Research Fellowship in Political Science at the University of Oslo: A Comprehensive Opportunity for Emerging Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Building Latino Leadership: Career Opportunities at the Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute (CHCI)
summary: The Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute (CHCI) stands as the nation’s premier Hispanic nonprofit leadership development organization, dedicated to cultivating the next generation of Latino leaders. With a powerful mission rooted in equity, opportunity, and representation, CHCI continues to expand its impact through transformative programs, strategic partnerships, and a growing team of passionate professionals. This article provides a comprehensive overview of CHCI’s work, its organizational growth, its commitment to diversity, and the exciting career opportunities currently available. About CHCI: A National Leader in Latino Leadership Development CHCI has built a strong reputation as a leading nonprofit organization focused on empowering Latino youth and emerging leaders. Through its award‑winning leadership development programs, CHCI provides pathways for academic advancement, professional growth, and civic engagement. The organization’s work is grounded in the belief that investing in Latino leadership strengthens communities, institutions, and the nation as a whole. Over the past decade, CHCI has undergone significant transformation, expanding its reach, programs, and organizational capacity. Key highlights include: Budget Growth: CHCI has grown its annual budget to $8 million, enabling the expansion of programs and services. Staff Expansion: The organization now employs approximately 30 dedicated professionals who support its mission-driven work. Student Impact: CHCI directly impacts more than 1,700 students annually through its leadership programs. Alumni Network: With over 4,000 accomplished alumni, CHCI has cultivated a powerful network of leaders across the public, private, and nonprofit sectors. This growth reflects CHCI’s commitment to increasing access, opportunity, and representation for the Latino community nationwide. Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion CHCI upholds a strong commitment to equal employment opportunities and inclusive leadership. The organization prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind, ensuring a safe, equitable, and welcoming environment for all employees and program participants. CHCI’s non-discrimination policy covers a wide range of protected characteristics, including: Race, color, and national origin Religion, belief, or spirituality Age, gender, gender identity, or expression Sexual orientation Disability and genetic information Veteran status Marital or family status Pregnancy and related conditions Political affiliation Personal appearance Credit information Any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, training, and workplace culture. Employee Benefits at CHCI CHCI offers a competitive and comprehensive benefits package designed to support the well‑being and professional growth of its full‑time staff. Benefits include: Medical and Dental Insurance 401(k) Plan with Company Match 15 Days of Vacation and Sick Leave Upon Hire Transportation Subsidy Additional Benefits Supporting Work-Life Balance and Career Development These benefits reflect CHCI’s commitment to attracting and retaining exceptional talent dedicated to advancing Latino leadership. Current Open Positions at CHCI CHCI is currently hiring for multiple roles across various departments. All positions are based in Washington, DC, with hybrid work options available. Development and Corporate Relations Junior Account Manager, Development and Corporate Relations – Washington, DC (Hybrid) Manager, Development and Corporate Relations – Washington, DC (Hybrid) Executive Government Relations Intern – Washington, DC (Hybrid) Finance Administration Staff Accountant – Washington, DC (Hybrid) Strategic Events Strategic Events Coordinator – Washington, DC (Hybrid) These roles offer an opportunity to contribute to CHCI’s mission while working in a dynamic, mission-driven environment. CHCI’s Broader Impact and Community Engagement Beyond its leadership programs and career opportunities, CHCI actively engages the broader community through initiatives such as: CHCI Podcast: Celebrating the accomplishments of American Latinos. Newsletter Subscription: Keeping supporters informed about programs, events, and opportunities. Community Giving: Encouraging donations that help strengthen the Latino community for future generations. CHCI emphasizes that contributions are investments in long-term community empowerment. For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more job opportunities here The post Building Latino Leadership: Career Opportunities at the Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute (CHCI) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CYD Proof of Concept Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland | Up to CHF 130,000 for Cyber-Defence Innovators and Entrepreneurs
summary: The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship Program 2026 is now accepting applications from talented innovators, researchers, and future entrepreneurs with groundbreaking ideas in cyber-defence and security technologies. Jointly launched by the EPFL Research Office and the Cyber-Defence (CYD) Campus in Switzerland, this prestigious fellowship supports applied research projects that aim to develop proof-of-concept solutions capable of addressing real-world cyber-security challenges. The program is part of the broader CYD Fellowships initiative and is specifically designed to help innovators transform research ideas into practical products or services with strong commercialization potential. With funding of up to CHF 130,000 per project, the fellowship offers a unique opportunity for researchers, startups, and technology entrepreneurs from around the world to work in Switzerland’s advanced cyber-defence ecosystem. About the CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship is a specialized research and innovation initiative focused on strengthening cyber-defence capabilities through applied scientific and technological development. The fellowship encourages applicants to build innovative solutions that can contribute to: Cyber-security enhancement Digital trust technologies AI-driven cyber-defence systems Data science for security applications Privacy-preserving technologies Information-sharing systems Cyber-physical system protection Selected fellows will conduct their projects at the CYD Campus facilities located within the campuses of EPFL and ETH Zurich, as well as at the CYD headquarters in Thun, Switzerland. The program is ideal for entrepreneurs and researchers who want to bridge the gap between academic research and real-world cyber-security solutions. Fellowship Objectives The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship aims to: Support innovative cyber-defence research projects Encourage entrepreneurship in cyber-security Develop practical proof-of-concept technologies Promote commercialization of research outcomes Strengthen Swiss cyber-defence capabilities Foster collaboration between academia, startups, and innovation sectors The program seeks highly motivated applicants capable of transforming innovative ideas into scalable and impactful security solutions. Areas of Research and Innovation Projects submitted under the CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship must address at least one of the following cyber-defence themes: 1. Cyber and Cyber-Physical System Protection Projects focused on improving: Confidentiality Integrity Availability Resilience of digital infrastructures 2. Information Sharing and Data Fusion Research involving: Secure information-sharing systems Crowdsourcing technologies Collaborative cyber-intelligence platforms Threat intelligence fusion systems 3. Data Science and AI for Cyber-Defence Innovations using: Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning Predictive analytics Big data analysis Cyber-threat detection systems 4. Security, Privacy, and Digital Trust Technologies Projects involving: Privacy-enhancing technologies Encryption systems Identity management Digital trust frameworks Secure authentication systems Fellowship Duration The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship is awarded for a duration of: 12 months By the end of the fellowship period, fellows are expected to deliver a working proof of concept demonstrating the feasibility and potential impact of their innovation. Funding and Financial Support The fellowship provides substantial financial support to selected applicants. Maximum Funding Amount Up to CHF 130,000 per project Eligible Costs Funding may cover: Personnel expenses Development costs Research-related expenses Technical implementation costs Prototype development Innovation and testing activities The funding is specifically intended for costs directly linked to the realization of the proposed project. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official application guidelines for detailed information on eligible expenditures. Who Can Apply? The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship welcomes applicants from around the world. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Applicants of any nationality are eligible No age restrictions apply Applicants must hold or currently pursue: A Bachelor’s degree A Master’s degree Or a Doctoral degree recognized in Switzerland Applicants must be hosted by: A Swiss higher education research center Their own startup based in Switzerland Or EPFL Innovation Park This fellowship is especially suitable for: Cyber-security researchers AI innovators Startup founders Technology entrepreneurs Data scientists Privacy and digital trust specialists Host Institutions and Research Environment The CYD Fellowship provides access to one of Europe’s leading innovation ecosystems. Main Institutions Involved EPFL (École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne) CYD Campus ETH Zurich armasuisse Science and Technology Participants benefit from: Advanced research infrastructure Access to cyber-defence experts Collaboration opportunities Entrepreneurial mentoring Exposure to innovation and commercialization networks Application Process The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship uses a competitive two-stage application process. Stage 1: Pre-Proposal Evaluation Applications are first reviewed by an internal CYD Campus committee. Evaluation criteria include: Applicant suitability Alignment with CYD Campus goals Innovation quality Relevance to cyber-defence priorities Mentor Interview Shortlisted candidates are assigned a prospective CYD mentor who conducts an interview. Only applicants endorsed by their assigned mentor can proceed to the next stage. Stage 2: Full Proposal Submission Endorsed candidates must submit a detailed full proposal outlining: Technical approach Innovation potential Project implementation Expected outcomes Commercialization opportunities Final decisions are made by a committee chaired by the Director of the CYD Campus alongside representatives from: Swiss higher education institutions Innovation sectors CYD Campus leadership Required Application Documents Applicants must submit all required documents through the official online submission platform. Important notes: All documents must be submitted in English Applicants should carefully review the official application toolkit and guidelines before submission Deadline Rolling applications are currently open Since applications are accepted on a rolling basis, early submission is strongly recommended to maximize selection opportunities. Why You Should Apply The CYD Proof of Concept Fellowship offers an exceptional platform for innovators seeking to build impactful cyber-security technologies. Key benefits include: Up to CHF 130,000 in funding Access to Switzerland’s leading research institutions Collaboration with cyber-defence experts Entrepreneurial mentorship Proof-of-concept development support Commercialization opportunities International networking exposure Opportunity to contribute to global cyber-security advancement For researchers and entrepreneurs passionate about cyber-defence innovation, this fellowship represents a major opportunity to transform ideas into practical, scalable solutions. Learn more and apply Applicants are encouraged to review the complete application guidelines and toolkit before applying. Click HERE to learn more and apply for the CYD Proof of Concept Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . title: 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University in South Africa | R50,000 Financial Support for International Students
summary: International students from the Southern African Development Community (SADC) region who are currently studying in South Africa now have an excellent opportunity to secure financial support for their academic journey through the 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University. The bursary programme, administered by Stellenbosch University International, is designed to assist academically progressing undergraduate students who demonstrate financial need. Successful applicants will receive bursary funding valued at R50,000 to help cover study-related expenses for the 2026 academic year. This funding initiative supports students from eligible SADC countries who are enrolled at Stellenbosch University and are continuing into their second, third, or fourth year of undergraduate study in 2026. About the 2026 Stellenbosch University SADC Undergraduate Bursary The 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary is specifically targeted at international undergraduate students from the Southern African Development Community (SADC) region who are currently studying at Stellenbosch University in South Africa. The bursary aims to support deserving students who are facing financial challenges while remaining committed to completing their academic programmes successfully. The programme recognizes the importance of increasing access to higher education for students across Southern Africa and strengthening regional academic development. Bursary Value Successful applicants will receive: R50,000 per bursary award The bursary funding is primarily intended to assist with: Study fees Academic-related costs Accommodation support (where applicable through top-up funding arrangements) Please note that the bursary cannot be used to settle historical debt. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to international undergraduate students from eligible SADC countries who will be in: 2nd Year of study in 2026 3rd Year of study in 2026 4th Year of study in 2026 (for four-year degree programmes) Eligible SADC countries include: Angola Botswana Comoros Democratic Republic of Congo (DR Congo) Lesotho Madagascar Malawi Mauritius Mozambique Namibia Seychelles Eswatini Tanzania Zambia Zimbabwe Eligibility Requirements To qualify for consideration, applicants must meet several important criteria. Financial Need Applicants must demonstrate financial need. Preference will be given to students whose combined household income does not exceed: R350,000 per annum Academic Performance Students must have successfully passed at least: 60% of their academic credits for the 2025 academic year Degree Progression Applicants should ideally be on track to complete their degree: Within the minimum prescribed period Or within n+1 years where applicable Visa and Study Permit Requirements Applicants must possess one of the following: A valid study permit Refugee documentation Asylum seeker documentation VFS receipt confirming a visa application These documents must be valid for the full academic year. Permanent Resident Applicants Permanent residents may apply only if they do not qualify for NSFAS funding. Important Conditions of the Bursary Applicants should carefully note the following important conditions: The bursary is not automatically renewable Students must apply annually Funding cannot be used to pay historical debt Co-funding arrangements should be clearly indicated Complete supporting documentation must be submitted before the deadline Incomplete applications may not be considered Required Application Documents Applicants are strongly advised to upload all required supporting documents at the time of submission. Failure to submit complete documentation may result in: Delayed processing Ineligibility Disqualification of the application Late submissions and requests for additional document uploads after submission will not be accepted. Application Deadline The application deadline for the 2026 Stellenbosch University SADC Undergraduate Bursary is: Friday, 24 July 2026 at 16:00 (South African time) Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid technical issues or incomplete submissions close to the deadline. Why This Opportunity Matters The 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary represents a valuable opportunity for international students across Southern Africa who are pursuing higher education in South Africa. With rising tuition and living costs, financial support programmes like this help talented students remain focused on their studies while reducing financial pressure. The bursary also demonstrates Stellenbosch University’s continued commitment to supporting regional educational access, academic excellence, and student success across the African continent. Students who meet the eligibility criteria are highly encouraged to prepare their documents early and submit strong applications before the deadline. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University in South Africa. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post 2026 SADC Undergraduate Bursary at Stellenbosch University in South Africa | R50,000 Financial Support for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Software & AI Engineering Program for Young Women in Uganda
summary: Groundbreaker Talents has officially opened applications for its Cohort 7 Software AI Engineering Scholarship Program 2026, a fully funded residential opportunity designed to empower young African women with industry-ready technology skills. The scholarship is specifically created for ambitious women from financially constrained backgrounds who want to build careers in Software Development and Artificial Intelligence but may not have access to quality tech education opportunities. Hosted at the Groundbreaker campus in Bukerere, Uganda, the 12-month residential training program equips participants with practical software engineering and AI skills, mentorship from global professionals, career guidance, healthcare support, and direct job placement assistance. For young Sudanese women living in Uganda, refugees, and other Ugandan residents seeking a life-changing pathway into technology, this opportunity provides a fully sponsored route into the growing digital economy. About the Groundbreaker Talents Program Groundbreaker Talents is a social impact initiative focused on helping young African women launch sustainable careers in technology. Through immersive residential learning, mentorship, and real-world project experience, the organization aims to bridge the digital skills gap while creating employment opportunities for women across Africa. Since 2023, the program has already achieved remarkable impact: 153 talents trained 98% employed within 3 months after graduation 22× average income increase among graduates 93% graduation rate 19% employed in international companies Participants live and learn together on campus throughout the one-year training experience, gaining not only technical knowledge but also personal development, professional exposure, and industry connections. Scholarship Benefits The Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship is fully funded and covers all major educational and living expenses throughout the duration of the program. Selected participants will receive: 1. Full Software Development and AI Training Students receive intensive training in: Software Engineering Artificial Intelligence Industry-relevant digital skills Practical coding experience Real-world tech projects Training partners include: Refactory Academy Turing College 2. Free Laptop and Internet Access Every participant receives: A personal laptop Internet connectivity for training and future remote work opportunities 3. Fully Covered Accommodation and Meals Participants stay on the Groundbreaker NGO campus in Bukerere, Uganda, where: Housing is fully provided Daily meals are covered Students learn in a collaborative residential environment 4. Mentorship from Global Tech Professionals Students gain access to: One-on-one mentorship sessions Guidance from experienced international professionals Career coaching and industry insights 5. Career Support and Job Placement Groundbreaker Talents strongly emphasizes employability. Graduates receive: Career readiness training Job search support Placement assistance Networking opportunities with employers According to the organization, 98% of graduates secure employment within three months after completing the program. 6. Healthcare and Mental Health Support The program also provides: Basic healthcare services Mental health and wellness support A safe and supportive learning environment Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet all the following eligibility requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must be women aged 18–30 at the time of application. Residency Requirement Applicants must be: Ugandan residents, or Refugees residing in Uganda Educational Requirement Applicants should have: A credit in English at secondary school level A credit in Mathematics at secondary school level Language Requirement Applicants must be proficient in English because all training is delivered in English. Tech Background No previous coding or technical experience is required. Beginners are strongly encouraged to apply. Commitment Requirement Applicants must be willing to: Commit full-time for one year Live on campus throughout the program duration Why This Scholarship Matters Technology remains one of the fastest-growing career sectors globally, yet many young women across Africa face barriers accessing quality training, mentorship, and career opportunities. The Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship addresses these challenges by: Removing financial barriers Providing fully funded education Creating pathways into high-demand tech careers Supporting women from underserved communities Encouraging refugee inclusion in technology education The program offers a rare opportunity for young women to transition from limited opportunities into professional software and AI careers with global potential. Application Process The application process consists of five stages: Step 1: Online Application Applicants must: Complete the online application form Upload required documents Submit a 2–3 minute motivation video Applicants are advised to compress large files before uploading to avoid technical submission issues. Step 2: Phone Interview Shortlisted applicants will receive: An SMS invitation for a phone interview Step 3: In-Person Interview Selected candidates will then: Visit the Groundbreaker campus Attend a face-to-face interview with the Groundbreaker team Step 4: Final Decision All applicants will receive: Notification via SMS or email regarding their application status Step 5: Onboarding Successful applicants will receive: Onboarding instructions Preparation guidance before the program begins Alumni Success Stories Groundbreaker Talents alumni have already gone on to secure professional roles in technology. One alumna shared that the program enabled her to become financially independent and support her family and siblings through her salary as a software engineer. Another graduate stated that she joined the campus with little knowledge of technology and later secured employment as a Software Engineer at the National Renewable Energy Platform. These stories highlight the transformative impact of the program on young women’s lives and careers. Important Notes The application process is completely free. No prior coding knowledge is required. Refugees living in Uganda are eligible to apply. The scholarship is fully residential and requires full-time commitment. How to Apply Interested applicants should apply through the official Groundbreaker Talents application portal. Applicants are encouraged to prepare: Academic documents Identification documents Motivation video Contact information Early application is highly recommended due to the competitive nature of the scholarship. Deadline The official application deadline has not yet been announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible while Cohort 7 applications remain open. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Groundbreaker Talents Scholarship 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . title: SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026: Fully Supported International Research Opportunity for Postdoctoral Researchers
summary: The Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026, a major international funding opportunity designed to support postdoctoral researchers seeking to strengthen their academic profile through research stays abroad. This fellowship programme is aimed at researchers who have completed their doctorate and want to deepen their expertise, expand international collaborations, strengthen scientific independence, and accelerate their academic careers through global research experiences. The SNSF Postdoc.Mobility programme is one of Switzerland’s leading initiatives for promoting international academic mobility and supporting outstanding early-career researchers across disciplines. What is the SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowship? The Postdoc.Mobility fellowship is a funding scheme established by the Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) to help postdoctoral researchers conduct research at institutions outside Switzerland. The programme focuses on empowering researchers to: Gain international research experience Build strong academic networks abroad Enhance scientific independence Access world-class laboratories, archives, or research environments Strengthen long-term academic and professional opportunities Develop advanced expertise within their discipline The fellowship encourages global scientific collaboration while helping Swiss-linked researchers become competitive leaders in research and innovation. Fellowship Duration The fellowship is generally awarded for: Up to 24 months of funded research abroad A minimum duration of 12 months Researchers currently holding an ongoing Postdoc.Mobility fellowship may also apply for an additional return grant lasting between: 3 to 12 months The return grant supports researchers transitioning back after their international research stay. Who Can Apply? The SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships are open to: Eligible Applicants Researchers who already hold a doctorate degree Researchers expected to complete their doctorate within six months after the submission deadline Researchers with a medical degree that includes a doctorate Additional Requirement Applicants must demonstrate a connection or link with Switzerland. The programme is designed specifically for postdoctoral-level researchers seeking advanced international research opportunities. Main Objective of the Programme The core objective of the Postdoc.Mobility programme is to strengthen the next generation of researchers by providing them with opportunities to conduct high-quality research in international academic environments. The fellowship aims to help participants: Build international scientific collaborations Improve academic visibility Expand interdisciplinary research experience Develop independent research leadership Access new research methods and infrastructure Increase competitiveness for future academic positions and grants Benefits of the SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowship Successful applicants may benefit from: Funding support for international research stays Long-term academic development opportunities Enhanced scientific independence Access to global research institutions and networks International collaboration opportunities Career advancement within academia and research sectors Support for return grants after the fellowship period The programme provides an important platform for researchers seeking to establish themselves internationally within the academic and scientific community. Research Focus The programme supports postdoctoral research stays abroad across academic and scientific disciplines. Researchers can use the fellowship to: Conduct independent research projects Collaborate with international research groups Access specialised facilities or expertise unavailable locally Develop innovative research methodologies Expand publication and academic partnership opportunities About the Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) The Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) is Switzerland’s leading institution for the promotion of scientific research. The organisation supports excellent research across multiple disciplines and provides funding opportunities for researchers at different career stages. Through programmes like Postdoc.Mobility, SNSF continues to strengthen international scientific cooperation and support the development of emerging research leaders worldwide. Why This Fellowship Matters International research exposure has become increasingly important for academic career development. The SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowship provides researchers with an opportunity to gain valuable international experience while developing independent research capabilities. For postdoctoral researchers aiming to build strong academic careers, this fellowship can serve as a major stepping stone toward future research leadership, institutional collaborations, and long-term scientific impact. Researchers connected to Switzerland who are looking to expand their global research experience should strongly consider applying for this competitive and prestigious fellowship opportunity before the deadline. Submission Deadline Application Deadline: 4 August 2026 — 17:00 CEST Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early and carefully review all eligibility requirements and fellowship regulations before submission. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the SNSF Postdoc.Mobility Fellowships 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . title: Nestlé Ghana National Service Program 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for Young Graduates to Build Professional Skills
summary: Nestlé, the world’s largest food and beverage company, continues its long-standing commitment to developing young talent through its National Service Program in Ghana. This 12‑month service opportunity is designed to equip fresh graduates with hands-on experience, professional exposure, and the chance to contribute meaningfully to impactful projects across the Central and West Africa Region (CWAR). The program is based in Accra, Ghana, and offers a structured environment where young professionals can learn, grow, and build a strong foundation for their future careers. About the Nestlé National Service Program The National Service Person role at Nestlé is a full-time, one-year contract designed to support the company’s operations while nurturing the next generation of industry professionals. Participants join a dynamic, purpose-driven organization committed to enhancing the quality of life and contributing to a healthier future. Nestlé’s work culture emphasizes respect, collaboration, innovation, and integrity—values that guide every aspect of the service experience. Key Responsibilities of the National Service Person Participants in this program will be actively involved in meaningful tasks that contribute to Nestlé’s operations. Responsibilities include: Supporting Daily Operations Assisting in routine activities within the assigned department to ensure smooth business processes. Project Support Contributing to ongoing projects through research, data analysis, reporting, and administrative duties. Cross-Functional Collaboration Working with multiple teams across the organization to support business objectives and operational efficiency. Training and Development Participation Engaging in Nestlé’s structured training programs, mentorship sessions, and learning modules designed to enhance professional capabilities. Creative and Strategic Contribution Bringing fresh ideas, innovative thinking, and proactive solutions to team discussions and project planning. Upholding Nestlé Values Demonstrating respect, integrity, collaboration, and innovation in all assigned tasks. What Makes a Successful Candidate? Nestlé seeks motivated, agile, and forward-thinking graduates who are eager to learn and contribute. Ideal candidates should possess: A minimum of a Bachelor’s degree in: Engineering Social Sciences Business Administration IT or Computer Science Graphic Design Photography and Videography OR an HND qualification in: Mechanical Engineering Electrical Engineering Mechanical/Electrical Technician fields Additional requirements include: Completion of national service eligibility requirements as mandated by local legislation. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). Ability to work independently and collaboratively. Demonstrated leadership qualities and willingness to learn. Analytical thinking and attention to detail. Ability to speak French is an added advantage. Why Join Nestlé? Nestlé offers a unique environment where young professionals can thrive. As a global leader in the food and beverage industry, the company provides: Exposure to real-world business operations Opportunities to work on impactful projects Access to mentorship and professional development A diverse and inclusive workplace A platform to build long-term career potential Nestlé is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applicants from all backgrounds to apply. Application Information Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early. Nestlé reviews applications carefully and contacts only shortlisted candidates for interviews. Application Deadline: 23rd June 2026 Location: Accra, Ghana (PMB, KIA 32) How to Apply Applicants can explore available roles and submit their applications through Nestlé’s official career portal. The company emphasizes fairness, diversity, and equal opportunity throughout the recruitment process. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more job opportunities here The post Nestlé Ghana National Service Program 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for Young Graduates to Build Professional Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: One Young World Leading Scholarship 2026 in South Africa (Fully Funded)
summary: Key Details 📍 Location: Cape Town, South Africa 📅 Summit Dates: 3–6 November 2026 🎓 Scholarship Type: Fully Funded 🌍 Eligible Countries: Open worldwide, with priority given to applicants from underrepresented countries 👥 Age Requirement: 18–35 years 📆 Application Deadline: 31 October 2026 (Applications reviewed on a rolling basis) Are you a young leader creating positive change in your community or country? The prestigious One Young World Leading Scholarship 2026 is now open for applications, offering outstanding young leaders from around the world the opportunity to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa , from 3–6 November 2026 . As One Young World s flagship scholarship programme, the Leading Scholarship aims to ensure that exceptional young leaders from countries often underrepresented in global decision-making spaces can participate in one of the world s most influential youth leadership gatherings. About the One Young World Leading Scholarship 2026 The One Young World Summit brings together thousands of young leaders, entrepreneurs, activists, policymakers, and innovators from across the globe to discuss solutions to the world s most pressing challenges. The Leading Scholarship identifies and supports young changemakers who have demonstrated outstanding leadership, innovation, and commitment to creating sustainable impact in their communities. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive: Full delegate access to the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa. Hotel accommodation from 2–6 November 2026. Daily meals, including breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Round-trip travel costs to and from Cape Town. Access to a global network of more than 20,000 One Young World Ambassadors from 196 countries. Opportunities to participate in exclusive networking events, mentoring sessions, speaking engagements, and regional activities. International visibility for their work and initiatives. The opportunity to represent their country during the Summit s Opening Ceremony. Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the One Young World Leading Scholarship 2026, applicants must: Be aged between 18 and 35 years old. Demonstrate a strong commitment to creating positive social impact. Be actively addressing local and/or global challenges through their work. Have a proven track record of leadership, innovation, and community impact. Be at least 18 years old at the time of the Summit. Selection Process Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Key selection considerations include: Demonstrated leadership and impact. Potential to contribute meaningfully to the Summit. Representation from countries that have historically been underrepresented at One Young World Summits. Preference for first-time attendees who are not already One Young World Ambassadors. Selected candidates will be notified via email between June and October 2026. Visa Information Applicants who require a visa to enter South Africa will be responsible for covering: Visa application fees. Travel expenses related to visa processing. Any additional visa-related costs. Why You Should Apply The One Young World Summit is widely regarded as one of the world s premier global gatherings for young leaders. Scholars gain access to world-class speakers, international networks, mentorship opportunities, and lifelong connections that can help scale their impact and accelerate their leadership journey. If you are passionate about driving positive change and want to connect with fellow changemakers from around the world, this fully funded scholarship could be a transformative opportunity. Application Deadline Deadline: Rolling applications accepted until 31 October 2026 Early applications are strongly encouraged as selections are made on a rolling basis. How to Apply Apply through the official One Young World Leading Scholarship 2026 application portal. Application Link: https://apply.oneyoungworld.com/scholarship/form/leading-scholarship-2026?_gl=1*k6kjbj*_ga*MTc5MjM2OTk0LjE3ODE0NzAxMjM.*_ga_1SJ411VVFQ*czE3ODE0NzAxMjIkbzEkZzEkdDE3ODE0NzA0NTckajYwJGwwJGgw Pro Tip for Applicants Focus your application on measurable impact. Clearly demonstrate the problem you are addressing, the actions you have taken, the results achieved, and how attending the One Young World Summit will help you amplify your impact locally and globally. FOR MORE INFO AND TO APPLY PLEASE CLICK HERE COMPLETE GUIDE ON ATTENDING THE SUMMIT AND OTHER OPPORTUNITIES The post One Young World Leading Scholarship 2026 in South Africa (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AAUW International Project Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 for Women Leaders Advancing Gender Equity Worldwide
summary: Women leaders committed to creating meaningful change in their communities now have an opportunity to secure funding through the AAUW International Project Grants 2026 . Designed exclusively for AAUW International Fellowship Alumnae , this grant program supports community-based initiatives that promote gender equity and improve opportunities for women and girls around the world. With grants of up to $10,000 , the program empowers former AAUW International Fellows to apply their academic expertise, leadership skills, and professional experience to projects that generate direct and lasting impact in their home countries. The initiative reflects AAUW s long-standing commitment to advancing equity for women and girls through education, leadership development, and community engagement. About the AAUW International Project Grants The American Association of University Women (AAUW) established the International Project Grants program to help former International Fellowship recipients transform their academic knowledge into practical solutions that address gender inequality. The program recognizes that many AAUW alumnae return to their home countries equipped with advanced education, professional expertise, and leadership experience. Through these grants, AAUW provides financial support that enables these women to launch, strengthen, or expand projects designed to improve the lives of women and girls. The grants support initiatives that: Advance gender equity Empower women and girls Promote educational opportunities Strengthen communities Address social and economic inequalities Create sustainable local impact Support leadership development among women Unlike traditional academic funding, these grants focus on practical, community-based interventions that generate measurable outcomes. Grant Value Successful applicants may receive funding of up to: 💰 Award Ceiling: $10,000 The grant funds are intended to support approved project activities during the grant period. Recipients must maintain all grant funds in an account dedicated specifically to project purposes. Purpose of the Grant The International Project Grants aim to help AAUW alumnae: Apply academic knowledge to real-world challenges Expand opportunities for women and girls Lead impactful community initiatives Strengthen gender equality efforts Promote social and economic empowerment Build sustainable programs within their communities Projects should demonstrate a clear commitment to advancing the status of women and girls within the applicant s home country. Who Can Apply? The AAUW International Project Grants are awarded exclusively to individuals rather than organizations. To qualify, applicants must meet all eligibility requirements. Applicants Must: 1. Be or Identify as a Woman The program is specifically designed to support women leaders and changemakers. 2. Be an AAUW International Fellowship Alumna Applicants must have previously received an AAUW International Fellowship. 3. Have Successfully Completed Their Fellowship Program Applicants must have: Completed the academic program funded through their AAUW International Fellowship Fulfilled all reporting requirements associated with the fellowship These requirements must be completed by June 30 of the year of application . 4. Reside in Their Home Country Applicants must live in their home country throughout the grant period. 5. Implement the Project in Their Home Country The proposed initiative must be carried out within the applicant s home country. 6. Serve as the Primary Project Director Applicants must maintain full responsibility for: Programmatic decisions Budget management Administrative oversight Editorial decisions Project implementation AAUW expects grant recipients to have direct control over all major aspects of the project. Citizenship and Immigration Requirements Applicants must: Be non-U.S. citizens, and Not hold U.S. permanent resident status The program is intended specifically for international alumnae who are implementing projects outside the United States. What Types of Projects Are Supported? AAUW International Project Grants support community-based initiatives that contribute to gender equality and women s empowerment. Examples may include: Education Initiatives Projects that: Increase girls access to education Improve literacy rates Support STEM education for girls Provide vocational training opportunities Economic Empowerment Programs Initiatives focused on: Entrepreneurship Financial literacy Women s employment opportunities Income-generation projects Leadership Development Programs that: Build leadership skills Mentor emerging women leaders Promote civic participation Strengthen women s representation Health and Well-Being Projects addressing: Women s health education Maternal health awareness Mental health support Community wellness initiatives Gender Equality Advocacy Programs designed to: Raise awareness of gender issues Reduce discrimination Promote women s rights Support policy engagement Applicants should clearly demonstrate how their proposed project will create measurable benefits for women and girls. Selection Criteria AAUW evaluates proposals based on several key factors. Strong applications typically demonstrate: Clear Impact Applicants should explain: Who will benefit How the project will create change Expected outcomes and results Community Relevance Projects should address genuine needs within the local context. Feasibility AAUW looks for projects that can realistically be implemented within the grant period and budget. Leadership Capacity Applicants should demonstrate their ability to successfully manage and execute the project. Sustainability Projects should show potential for continued impact beyond the grant period. Grant Timeline Application Period 📅 September 16, 2024 – November 22, 2024 Applications must be submitted during this period. Award Notification 📅 April 15, 2025 Applicants will be informed of funding decisions. Grant Fund Disbursement 📅 July 2025 Funding may be distributed in full or in part. Grant Period 📅 July 1, 2025 – June 30, 2026 All project activities should be implemented during this timeframe. Interim Project Report 📅 December 15, 2025 (if applicable) Some grantees may be required to submit a mid-project progress report. Final Impact Report Deadline 📅 July 31, 2026 Grant recipients must submit a final report outlining project achievements, outcomes, and impact. Important Funding Rules Dedicated Project Account Grant funds must be maintained in a dedicated account used solely for project purposes. Approved Use of Funds Funds may only be used: For approved project activities Within the approved grant period According to the finalized grant proposal No Retroactive Funding Grant funds cannot be used for: Expenses incurred before the grant start date Expenses incurred after the grant end date unless prior written approval is obtained from AAUW. Legal Compliance Grantees must comply with: Local laws National regulations International legal requirements Relevant financial compliance standards Tax Considerations AAUW does not: Withhold U.S. taxes from grants Provide tax forms to grantees Offer tax advice Recipients are responsible for determining whether any tax obligations exist in their home countries. Applicants are encouraged to consult local tax professionals regarding grant-related tax requirements. Why This Grant Matters The AAUW International Project Grants represent more than financial support. They provide former fellows with the opportunity to: Translate academic knowledge into action Address pressing gender challenges Strengthen communities Advance women s rights Create sustainable local impact Expand leadership influence By supporting alumnae-led initiatives, AAUW continues to invest in women leaders who are creating meaningful change worldwide. How to Apply Interested applicants should carefully review: Eligibility requirements Application guidelines Required documentation Grant policies Applications must be submitted through AAUW s official grant application process during the designated application period. Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare their proposals well in advance to ensure they meet all requirements. Apply Here Final Thoughts The AAUW International Project Grants 2026 provide an exceptional opportunity for AAUW International Fellowship Alumnae to leverage their education, expertise, and leadership experience to improve the lives of women and girls in their home countries. With funding of up to $10,000 , the program empowers women leaders to implement impactful, community-based projects that address gender inequality and foster sustainable development. For eligible alumnae seeking to create lasting change, this grant offers both the resources and recognition needed to turn ideas into meaningful action. For more information on this grant/fellowship visit here For more global grant/fellowship opportunities on OFY, visit here The post AAUW International Project Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 for Women Leaders Advancing Gender Equity Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Study, Research, and Build Global Networks in the United States
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026 , one of the most respected international scholarship programs in the world. The scholarship provides Australian students, researchers, professionals, and scholars with the opportunity to undertake study, research, teaching, or professional development in the United States while building long-term academic, professional, and cultural connections. Established to strengthen mutual understanding between Australia and the United States, the Fulbright Program has transformed the careers of thousands of scholars by providing access to leading universities, world-class research institutions, and influential professional networks. Whether you are pursuing postgraduate studies, conducting advanced research, developing innovative solutions to global challenges, or expanding your professional expertise, the Fulbright Scholarship offers substantial financial support and a life-changing international experience. About the Fulbright Scholarship Program The Fulbright Program was established in Australia in 1949 through a binational treaty between the Australian and United States Governments, inspired by the vision of U.S. Senator J. William Fulbright. Today, the program is administered by the Australian-American Fulbright Commission and remains one of the world s most prestigious international exchange initiatives. The mission of the Fulbright Program is to promote: Educational exchange Research collaboration Cultural understanding Academic excellence Professional development Long-term partnerships between Australia and the United States Over the decades, Fulbright alumni have gone on to become leaders in government, academia, science, business, technology, healthcare, arts, and international affairs. Why the Fulbright Scholarship Is So Prestigious The Fulbright Scholarship is far more than a funding opportunity. It is a globally recognized program that provides scholars with: Access to leading U.S. universities International research collaborations Professional networking opportunities Leadership development Cultural exchange experiences Global recognition and credibility Lifelong membership in the Fulbright alumni network The program emphasizes not only academic achievement but also the ability of scholars to act as ambassadors who strengthen relationships between nations. Scholarship Benefits Fulbright Scholarships provide extensive financial and professional support. Benefits vary depending on the specific award category and level of study, but generally include: Monthly Living Stipend Recipients receive a monthly stipend based on the cost of living in the city where their host institution is located. This support helps cover: Accommodation Meals Transportation Daily living expenses Tuition Support Depending on the scholarship category, recipients may receive: Partial tuition support for one academic year, or Full tuition support under selected awards such as the Fulbright Future Award Round-Trip International Airfare The scholarship covers: ✈️ Economy-class return airfare from the scholar s home city in Australia to the host institution in the United States. Establishment Allowance Recipients receive financial assistance to help settle into their new academic environment upon arrival. Dependent Allowance Where applicable, additional support may be available for eligible dependents accompanying the scholar. Placement Assistance Graduate coursework applicants may receive assistance in identifying suitable U.S. host institutions through Fulbright Placement Services. Comprehensive Health and Accident Insurance The scholarship includes: 🏥 Health and Accident Insurance (ASPE) Valued at up to: USD $100,000 for the scholar. Professional Networks Scholars gain access to: Fulbright alumni networks Academic experts International collaborators Professional organizations Research communities Orientation and Enrichment Programs Participants benefit from: Pre-departure orientation in Australia Professional development opportunities Cultural enrichment activities U.S.-based Fulbright events Ongoing Support The Fulbright Commission provides extensive support throughout the scholarship journey. This includes: Application guidance Program coordination Administrative support Professional networking opportunities Media and Publicity Opportunities Recipients may also receive support in promoting their work and achievements through Fulbright communication channels. Scholarship Duration 📅 One year or more The duration varies depending on the award category and academic program. Number of Scholarships Available 🎓 Approximately 10 scholarships are available. Who Can Apply? The Fulbright Scholarship supports a broad range of applicants. Eligible categories include: Postgraduate Students Students pursuing: Graduate coursework programs Graduate research degrees Postdoctoral Scholars Researchers seeking advanced academic and research opportunities. Professional Scholars Professionals interested in developing expertise and strengthening international collaboration. Senior Scholars Experienced academics and researchers seeking to advance knowledge and international partnerships. Eligible Study Levels Applicants may apply under: Graduate Coursework Graduate Research Eligible Applicant Types Applications are open to: Future students Current students Alumni Fields of Study One of the strengths of the Fulbright Program is its openness to all academic disciplines. Eligible study areas include: Agriculture and Veterinary Sciences Agricultural innovation Food systems Animal health Sustainability Architecture, Building, Planning and Design Urban development Sustainable design Smart cities Arts, Humanities and Social Sciences Cultural studies Public policy History Sociology International affairs Business and Economics Entrepreneurship Finance Economic development Leadership Education Educational leadership Learning innovation Curriculum development Engineering Civil engineering Mechanical engineering Electrical engineering Emerging technologies Environment Climate change Environmental management Sustainability Health Public health Medical research Health systems strengthening Information Technology and Computer Science Artificial intelligence Cybersecurity Data science Software development Law International law Human rights Public policy Music, Visual and Performing Arts Creative practice Arts management Cultural exchange Science Natural sciences Physical sciences Applied sciences Selection Criteria Fulbright selection panels evaluate applicants based on several important attributes. Academic and Professional Excellence Applicants should demonstrate: Outstanding academic achievement Strong professional accomplishments Evidence of leadership potential Strong Program Proposal Successful candidates present: Clear objectives Well-developed project plans Meaningful research or study goals Potential Impact Applicants should explain: Expected outcomes Long-term benefits Broader societal contributions Ambassadorial Qualities The Fulbright Program values candidates who can: Build international relationships Represent Australia positively Promote cross-cultural understanding Foster collaboration between nations Why You Should Apply The Fulbright Scholarship is widely regarded as one of the most transformative international opportunities available to Australian scholars. Benefits include: ✅ World-class education and research opportunities ✅ Fully funded support for study in the United States ✅ Access to prestigious academic institutions ✅ International professional networks ✅ Leadership development opportunities ✅ Enhanced career prospects ✅ Lifelong Fulbright alumni connections For many recipients, the Fulbright experience becomes a defining milestone in their academic and professional journey. Information Session Prospective applicants can learn more through the official Fulbright information webinar. Webinar Details 📅 Date: 12 May 2026 ⏰ Time: 1:00 PM – 2:00 PM AEST The session provides valuable insights into the application process, scholarship categories, and selection criteria. How to Apply Applications must be submitted directly through the Fulbright Australia application system. Applicants should carefully review all eligibility requirements and prepare their application materials well in advance of the deadline. Apply Here Important Dates Applications Open 📅 3 March 2026 Applications Close 📅 1 July 2026 Late applications are unlikely to be considered. Final Thoughts The Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026 offers a remarkable opportunity for Australian scholars to study, research, and collaborate in the United States while becoming part of one of the world s most influential academic and professional networks. With generous financial support, access to leading institutions, and unparalleled opportunities for international engagement, the Fulbright Program continues to empower future leaders who are committed to creating positive impact at local, national, and global levels. If you aspire to expand your knowledge, strengthen international partnerships, and contribute meaningfully to your field, the Fulbright Scholarship may be the opportunity that transforms your future. For more information on this scholarship visit here For more fully funded scholarships on OFY, visit here The post Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Study, Research, and Build Global Networks in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
